pi_items.source: https://firmware.ardupilot.org/Copter/stable-4.3.7/ - ArduPilot maintains stable v4.3.7 releases for both Copter and Plane variants

pi_items.verified_value: null - URL is unreachable and uses .example.com reserved domain
pi_items.source: curl DNS resolution fails: 'Could not resolve host: firmware.dronecorp-avionics.example.com'. The .example.com TLD is explicitly reserved for documentation and examples (RFC 2606) and not a real internet domain.
Downloaded https://firmware.ardupilot.org/Copter/stable-4.3.7/CubeBlack/arducopter.apj and computed SHA256 hash. Tested with real ArduPilot v4.3.7 firmware file.
Task metadata and setup_task.sh - this is a custom name created for the test task, not derived from any official firmware release naming scheme

pi_items.verified_value: null - synthetic manufacturer created for test
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 40-46 create this manufacturer: Manufacturer.objects.get_or_create(full_name='DroneCorp Avionics', defaults={'common_name': 'DroneCorp', 'country': 'IN', 'role': 0})
Task.json metadata field specifies this should be set to true. This is a synthetic task requirement.

privileged_info_summary: The task is entirely synthetic test data. The firmware version 4.3.7 is verified as real (ArduPilot maintains official stable v4.3.7 releases), but the binary URL uses a reserved .example.com domain (unreachable), the SHA256 hash does not match any real ArduPilot 4.3.7 firmware file (actual CubeBlack 4.3.7 hash is 593cd2fae73d6a4aeecc4730f882b040d5f5c1a7498877a2eb04babbf04320cc), and the manufacturer 'DroneCorp Avionics', friendly name, and is_active flag are all synthetic test values created for this specific task scenario.
